pattern: Two cities are linked whenever they share a divisor above the threshold, so union every city under each of its multiples for divisors past the threshold and every query collapses to a single find() comparison.
hints: Instead of thinking pairwise, think per divisor: for a fixed divisor z above the threshold, every multiple of z should end up in the same connected group.
Use a union-find structure over the n cities; for each z from threshold+1 up to n, union z with 2z, 3z, 4z, ... so all of z's multiples join z's group.
After all the unions are done, each query is answered in near-constant time by checking whether the two cities' find() roots match.

edge_inputs.description: only 3 and 6 share a divisor above the threshold
only one pair shares a divisor above the threshold, rest are isolated
smallest n, threshold excludes the only shared divisor
same pair queried with arguments swapped, both directions must agree
threshold equal to n leaves no valid divisor above it, so nothing is connected

stress.description: random n, threshold and queries up to a large size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 10000))
    threshold = rng.randint(0, n)
    m = min(500, n)
    queries = []
    for _ in range(m):
        a = rng.randint(1, n)
        b = rng.randint(1, n)
        while b == a:
            b = rng.randint(1, n)
        queries.append([a, b])
    return [n, threshold, queries]
